statement: Every material UI, README, and demo claim resolves to a stable redacted artifact or deterministic assertion.

A checked-in v1 historical run contains only README.md and evidence-bundle.json. The portable bundle embeds its manifest plus the task, Git stack, base and current blueprints, stack-wide delta, per-review-commit GraphReFly snapshots and Mermaid projections, per-work-unit structural deltas and raw diffs, ChangePlan, stale and final semantic records, before-change and after-change GateResults, selective replan, checks, final GateResult, separately attributed review decision, and selected redacted live provenance. Expanded artifact names are logical manifest paths and are never required as checked-in files.

Evidence covers current-valid, clean-rebase-semantic-stale, unrelated-change-still-valid, stale-parent, forged-or-wrong-scope, and fresh-selective-replan cases with exact attribution and reason codes.

The manifest identifies run ID, UTC time, source commit, Git object IDs, canonical input digest, schema and prompt versions, exact model ID and reasoning effort, BlueprintProvider ID and capabilities, GraphReFly version, policy revision, and artifact hashes; its digest rules follow C8 and never create a self-reference.
Commit reviewed redacted fixtures; keep raw provider responses under an ignored raw directory; preserve a replay path that requires no API key.
Never commit API keys, credentials, billing details, unredacted sensitive user content, private submission data, or large raw captures.
Each milestone records its demonstrated claim, command or user path, gate output, commit SHA, artifact paths, known limitations, and follow-up.

GateResult is a deterministic, read-only, reproducible verdict computed from current repository state, accepted SemanticChangeRecords, freshness witnesses, policy, and check evidence; UI actions may rerun checks or produce new evidence but may not edit the verdict.

A model or human claim is editable intent, not evidence. It becomes an accepted SemanticChangeRecord only after deterministic schema, source-scope, dependency, witness, and policy validation; an approval is a separately attributed evidence item.

The local CLI is the canonical gate runner. A future GitHub Actions workflow, GitHub App, or MCP adapter may invoke the same versioned gate function but may not redefine its inputs, policy, reason codes, or verdict semantics.

Every artifact sent from a repository-owned runner to a hosted surface records its schema, content digest, redaction policy revision, included artifact allowlist, omitted sensitive classes, and runner provenance; tests prove that raw source, unredacted GraphBlueprints, raw provider output, and credentials are excluded by default.

The first usable product evidence runs the same public repository-review command against at least two materially different compatible repositories, including one non-flagship small repository, and records discovered Git lineage, target @graphrefly/ts version, configured entrypoint, per-commit Blueprint hashes and deltas, structured code diffs, failure behavior, and browser-visible synchronization without fixture-only branches in the command path.

PRODUCT-CI evidence includes strict schema and golden-byte tests for CI invocation and result artifacts; a deterministic GitHub event harness covering pass, architecture-stale blocked, unrelated rebind, gate error, stale base or head, zero or ambiguous plan, malformed event, fork least privilege, dependency lifecycle-script requirement, missing sandbox, cancellation, rerun and tamper; packed-package conformance proving the generated workflow invokes the repository-pinned engine and uploads only the verified redacted bundle; local-versus-CI equality for GateInput digest, GateResult, WorkUnit order and reason codes; and at least one explicitly authorized real GitHub pull-request required-job run in an independent compatible repository. The evidence must state that merge queues, Check Runs, signed identity and hosted persistence are not claimed.

PRODUCT-HOSTED evidence covers public and private selected repositories and pass, blocked and error envelopes through the separate no-checkout OIDC sync path; proves exact issuer, audience, repository, installation, workflow, run, attempt, actor and revision binding; recomputes every canonical and nested artifact digest; rejects forged, expired, replayed, cross-tenant, revoked, stale, malformed, source-bearing, profile-widened, oversized, over-rate, over-storage and tampered requests; proves viewer, reviewer, admin and owner authorization plus fail-closed provider revalidation; verifies authenticated append-only decisions never edit GateResult and unverified local identity is not upgraded; exercises retention, immediate read denial, primary and backup deletion boundaries, audit export, outage, idempotent retry and expired-artifact UX; and runs the complete stack-qa gate. Evidence must identify the exact deployed or hermetic control-plane build, storage adapters, policy revision, region and remaining provider, residency, BYOK, billing, collaboration, topology and submission limits.

PRODUCT-COLLAB evidence uses strict canonical candidate and result bytes plus independent real Git repositories to cover: disjoint compatible changes; compatible changes that share a node but affect independent fields or relations; same-node incompatible values; node delete versus edge or claim use; edge add versus removal; subgraph mount and metadata-field conflicts; text conflict; candidate Blueprint diagnostics, execution or hash failure; accepted claim, dependency and policy invalidation; ambiguous merge base; non-linear contributor range; target and head movement; malformed, forged and tampered artifacts; fixed reason and witness ordering; and exact local-to-CI equality. It proves the source repository worktree, refs and commits are unchanged; IntegrationResult never edits GateResult or merges; target movement makes old success stale; recovery through rebase, implementation change or selective replan produces new evidence; overlap alone does not block; packed-package conformance works in materially different compatible repositories; the complete stack-qa gate passes; and reservations, actor credentials, pre-implementation prediction, merge queues, arbitrary DAGs, automatic merge and STACK-8 are not claimed.

PRODUCT-DAG evidence uses strict canonical v2 topology, binding, record, gate and review bytes plus materially different independent real Git repositories to cover: a normal branched stack and clean binary join; independent branches with stable records; architecture change with selective semantic dependency invalidation; join graph and policy conflict; exact per-parent Blueprint and code witnesses; dependency cycle, missing dependency, duplicate WorkUnit binding and dependency-not-ancestor errors; ambiguous merge base; merge WorkUnit trailer; manual-resolution tree mismatch; octopus merge; range and width overflow; Blueprint execution, hash and delta failure; fixed partial-order, reason and witness ordering under repeated and concurrent execution; rebase, cherry-pick, implementation and selective-replan recovery; cache invalidation boundaries; malformed, forged, reordered and tampered artifacts; explicit valid linear v1 conversion with unchanged v1 verification; local CLI and review parity; bounded merge_group equality and stale-event failure; packed-package conformance; unchanged source worktree, refs and commits; and the complete stack-qa gate. Evidence states the exact @graphrefly/ts and Blueprint versions and does not claim manual merge-resolution attribution, octopus support, larger bounds, queue management, automatic merge, rollback, reservations or STACK-8.

The bounded merge-group proof uses strict canonical bytes and independent verification to cover one and multiple Plans, repeated WorkUnit names under distinct Plan identities, repository policy that preexists and is shared by independently accepted Plans, explicit legacy conversion, exact checks_requested base and synthetic head identity, event-specific checkout and concurrency, canonical one-to-eight Plan ordering, per-Plan GateResult evaluation against the same final Blueprint evidence, compatible shared identities, cross-Plan graph and policy conflict, real invalid join witnesses, missing or duplicate Plan and WorkUnit trailers, ambiguous ownership, cross-Plan dependency rejection, stale or rebuilt groups, unsupported merge-free rebase or squash shape, over-limit groups, malformed and tampered aggregate artifacts, unchanged nested reasons, local-to-adapter byte equality, no source or unredacted Blueprint upload, unchanged repository state and packed-package conformance. Evidence must state that GitHub or hosted APIs do not supply verdict ownership, hosted DAG sync is absent, only clean binary merge topology is supported, and queue management, automatic merge, rollback and STACK-8 are not claimed.

PRODUCT-ROLLBACK evidence uses strict canonical recovery v1 bytes and independent verification in materially different real Git repositories to cover exact WorkUnit and whole-Plan targets; repeated WorkUnit names across Plans; reverse-dependant closure and unaffected exclusion witnesses; pure inverse, pure compensation and mixed recovery-step DAGs; clean one-parent and clean binary-join histories; ambiguous merge mainline, inverse conflict and later implementation drift; policy, head and lineage staleness; incomplete dependant disposition and unresolved external effects; explicit local authorization, wrong digest, wrong ref namespace and hosted-decision rejection; isolated branch creation with an unchanged caller worktree and refs outside the authorized recovery ref; partial failure, exact resume and non-destructive abort; malformed, forged, reordered and tampered attempt chains; portable evidence; unchanged nested DAG reasons; passing recovered and truthful blocked or error results; repeated deterministic execution; packed-package conformance; and complete stack-qa. Evidence states the exact GraphReFly and Blueprint versions and proves that Git success never supplies semantic recovery, existing semantic v1, DAG v2 and D43 bytes remain unchanged, and release targets, external execution, force updates, push, merge, automatic cleanup and STACK-8 are not claimed.

PRODUCT-SIMPLIFY evidence covers normal, broad-refactor, unexpected-reach, failed-promise, architecture-stale, structural-only, exactly-one-plan and ambiguous-plan review paths in compatible real repositories; proves that primary UI decisions require only Intent, Reach and Readiness while exact accepted scopes, changed paths, Blueprint effects, canonical reasons and checks remain traceable in technical details; proves the browser never executes or translates the gate and capability labels are not presented as runtime enforcement; verifies selected-commit scrolling and responsive behavior; preserves source worktree, refs and all existing artifact bytes; exercises the installed packed package and complete stack-qa; and states that arbitrary filesystem prevention, silent policy derivation, automatic approval or merge and all STACK-8 work are not claimed.
